name: Plugin Management with lazy.nvim
description: Install, configure, update, and lazy-load Neovim plugins efficiently using the lazy.nvim package manager.

steps.title: Add a new plugin
steps.description: Create a plugin spec file to install and configure a new plugin.
steps.tip: Each file in lua/plugins/ can return one spec table or a list of specs. lazy.nvim auto-discovers them.

steps.title: Sync plugins after changes
steps.description: Tell lazy.nvim to install new plugins and update existing ones.

steps.title: Configure lazy-loading triggers
steps.description: Set up plugins to load only when needed to keep startup fast.
steps.command:
cat > ~/.config/nvim/lua/plugins/git.lua << 'EOF'
return {
  { "lewis6991/gitsigns.nvim",
    event = { "BufReadPre", "BufNewFile" },
    config = true },
  { "tpope/vim-fugitive",
    cmd = { "Git", "Gvdiffsplit", "Gblame" } },
}
EOF
steps.tip: Use 'event' to load on buffer events, 'cmd' to load on command use, 'keys' to load on keybinding, or 'ft' to load on filetype.

steps.title: Check plugin load times
steps.description: Profile startup to see how long each plugin takes to load.

steps.title: Pin plugin versions with the lockfile
steps.description: Commit the lockfile to ensure reproducible plugin versions across machines.
steps.tip: The lockfile is updated automatically on :Lazy sync. Commit it to version control and run :Lazy restore on another machine.

fullScript:
# lazy.nvim Plugin Management Quick Reference
# (Run these commands inside Neovim)

# Open lazy.nvim UI
:Lazy

# Install/update all plugins
:Lazy sync

# Update plugins only
:Lazy update

# Restore from lockfile
:Lazy restore

# View startup profile
:Lazy profile

# Check for issues
:Lazy health

# Clean unused plugins
:Lazy clean

# View plugin logs
:Lazy log

# Lazy-loading triggers:
# event = "BufReadPre"     Load on buffer read
# cmd = "Telescope"        Load on command use
# keys = "<leader>ff"      Load on keybinding
# ft = "python"            Load on filetype

faq.question: How do I organize plugin specs across multiple files?
faq.answer: Put each plugin or group of related plugins in a separate file under lua/plugins/. lazy.nvim automatically loads all files in that directory when you call require('lazy').setup('plugins').

faq.question: How do I disable a plugin temporarily?
faq.answer: Add 'enabled = false' to the plugin spec: { 'plugin/name', enabled = false }. The plugin stays in the lockfile but is not loaded or updated.

faq.question: What if two plugins conflict with each other?
faq.answer: Use the 'priority' field to control load order (higher loads first, default is 50). Use the 'dependencies' field to ensure one plugin loads before another. Check :Lazy log for errors.
